task: How many High Energy Physics - Lattice articles listed in January 2020 on Arxiv had ps versions available?

trajectory.action:
file_saver({"content": "Title: Counting Criteria for PostScript (PS) Availability — arXiv hep-lat (January 2020)

1) Scope and Objective
- Goal: Count how many High Energy Physics — Lattice (hep-lat) articles listed in January 2020 on arXiv had PostScript (PS) versions available.
- Target set: All items listed on the monthly index page https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2001 (January 2020), including both “new” submissions and “cross-lists” that appear on this page.
- Counting unit: Each unique arXiv identifier (e.g., 2001.xxxxx). Count each article once.

2) Time Window and Listing Source
- Time window: January 1–31, 2020.
- Source of record: arXiv monthly listing page for hep-lat, January 2020: https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2001. This page defines the complete set of “articles listed in January 2020.”

3) Definition of “PS Version Available”
An article is counted as having a PostScript (PS) version available if any of the following conditions are met on arXiv:
- The article’s “Other formats” page (https://arxiv.org/formats/{identifier}) lists a working link labeled “PostScript” or “PS”, typically pointing to:
  • https://arxiv.org/ps/{identifier}
  • https://arxiv.org/ps/{identifier}vN (versioned)
- The “Other formats” page lists a compressed PostScript link (gzipped), e.g. “PostScript (gzipped)” with URL patterns like:
  • https://arxiv.org/ps/{identifier}.gz
  • https://arxiv.org/ps/{identifier}vN.gz
- Occasionally, a direct PostScript link may appear on the abstract page; presence of such a link also qualifies.

Notes:
- Presence of the PostScript link on the formats page is sufficient to count as “available.” We will not require downloading to verify integrity unless a link clearly returns an error.
- Links to other formats (PDF, DVI, source) do not count toward PS availability unless a specific PostScript link is present.

4) Inclusions and Exclusions
- Include: All records shown on https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2001 (both primary hep-lat and cross-lists that appear on this page).
- Exclude: Items not appearing on that page; duplicate counting of the same arXiv identifier; withdrawn entries are included only if they appear on the January 2020 listing and have PS links available at the time of checking.

5) Handling Edge Cases
- Multiple versions: Count based on any version offering a PS link (if v1 lacks PS but v2 has PS, count as available; we are evaluating current availability when checked).
- Broken links: If a PS link is present but returns a clear error on attempt, document the case; default rule is to count presence unless broken links are systematically observed.
- Redirects: If PostScript links redirect to an equivalent resource (e.g., via versioned canonicalization), still count as PS available.

6) Operational Procedure (for subsequent steps)
- Step 1: Extract all arXiv IDs from https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2001.
- Step 2: For each ID, open https://arxiv.org/formats/{identifier} and check for presence of “PostScript” or “PostScript (gzipped)” links.
- Step 3: Aggregate binary availability per ID and compute total count; perform spot checks for link functionality as needed.
- Output: Provide final count and a brief methodology summary.
", "file_path": "work_space/gaia_a7feb290_20251222_032005_068193/criteria_hep-lat_ps_availability_step0.md", "mode": "w"})

    print('Check exists:', p, p.exists())
    if p.exists():
        chosen = p
        break
if not chosen:
    raise FileNotFoundError('Could not find the 2020-01 html file in expected locations')
text = open(chosen,'r',encoding='utf-8').read()
ids = re.findall(r'arXiv:(\d{4}\.\d{5})', text)
uniq_ids = []
seen = set()
for i in ids:
    if i not in seen:
        seen.add(i)
        uniq_ids.append(i)
print('Total IDs extracted:', len(uniq_ids))
print('First 10 IDs:', uniq_ids[:10])
# Save JSONL
data_dir = Path(base)/'data'
data_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
jsonl_path = data_dir/'hep-lat_2020-01_arxiv_ids.jsonl'
with open(jsonl_path,'w',encoding='utf-8') as f:
    for aid in uniq_ids:
        rec = {
            'arxiv_id': aid,
            'abs_url': f'https://arxiv.org/abs/{aid}',
            'formats_url': f'https://arxiv.org/format/{aid}'
        }
        f.write(json.dumps(rec, ensure_ascii=False)+'\n')
print('Saved JSONL to', jsonl_path)
# Create summary per the rules (single consolidated file)
reports_dir = Path(base)/'reports'
reports_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
sum_path = reports_dir/'search_results_summary_step1.md'
# Extract the total line from text
m = re.search(r'Authors and titles for January 2020\s+Total of (\d+) entries', text)
header_line = f'Total of {m.group(1)} entries' if m else 'Total of N/A entries'
content = []
content.append('Information from hep-lat January 2020 via arXiv')
content.append('Sources:')
content.append('- https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2020-01?show=2000')
content.append('Extracted Content:')
content.append(f'- "{header_line}"')
content.append('- arXiv IDs found (unique):')
for aid in uniq_ids:
    content.append(f'  - {aid}')
open(sum_path,'w',encoding='utf-8').write('\n'.join(content))
print('Saved summary to', sum_path)


Title: Counting Criteria for PostScript (PS) Availability — arXiv hep-lat (January 2020)

1) Scope and Objective
- Goal: Count how many High Energy Physics — Lattice (hep-lat) articles listed in January 2020 on arXiv had PostScript (PS) versions available.
- Target set: All items listed on the monthly index page https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2001 (January 2020), including both “new” submissions and “cross-lists” that appear on this page.
- Counting unit: Each unique arXiv identifier (e.g., 2001.xxxxx). Count each article once.

2) Time Window and Listing Source
- Time window: January 1–31, 2020.
- Source of record: arXiv monthly listing page for hep-lat, January 2020: https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2001. This page defines the complete set of “articles listed in January 2020.”

3) Definition of “PS Version Available”
An article is counted as having a PostScript (PS) version available if any of the following conditions are met on arXiv:
- The article’s “Other formats” page (https://arxiv.org/formats/{identifier}) lists a working link labeled “PostScript” or “PS”, typically pointing to:
  • https://arxiv.org/ps/{identifier}
  • https://arxiv.org/ps/{identifier}vN (versioned)
- The “Other formats” page lists a compressed PostScript link (gzipped), e.g. “PostScript (gzipped)” with URL patterns like:
  • https://arxiv.org/ps/{identifier}.gz
  • https://arxiv.org/ps/{identifier}vN.gz
- Occasionally, a direct PostScript link may appear on the abstract page; presence of such a link also qualifies.

Notes:
- Presence of the PostScript link on the formats page is sufficient to count as “available.” We will not require downloading to verify integrity unless a link clearly returns an error.
- Links to other formats (PDF, DVI, source) do not count toward PS availability unless a specific PostScript link is present.

4) Inclusions and Exclusions
- Include: All records shown on https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2001 (both primary hep-lat and cross-lists that appear on this page).
- Exclude: Items not appearing on that page; duplicate counting of the same arXiv identifier; withdrawn entries are included only if they appear on the January 2020 listing and have PS links available at the time of checking.

5) Handling Edge Cases
- Multiple versions: Count based on any version offering a PS link (if v1 lacks PS but v2 has PS, count as available; we are evaluating current availability when checked).
- Broken links: If a PS link is present but returns a clear error on attempt, document the case; default rule is to count presence unless broken links are systematically observed.
- Redirects: If PostScript links redirect to an equivalent resource (e.g., via versioned canonicalization), still count as PS available.

6) Operational Procedure (for subsequent steps)
- Step 1: Extract all arXiv IDs from https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2001.
- Step 2: For each ID, open https://arxiv.org/formats/{identifier} and check for presence of “PostScript” or “PostScript (gzipped)” links.
- Step 3: Aggregate binary availability per ID and compute total count; perform spot checks for link functionality as needed.
- Output: Provide final count and a brief methodology summary.

Title: Counting Criteria for PostScript (PS) Availability — arXiv hep-lat (January 2020)

1) Scope and Objective
- Goal: Count how many High Energy Physics — Lattice (hep-lat) articles listed in January 2020 on arXiv had PostScript (PS) versions available.
- Target set: All items listed on the monthly index page https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2001 (January 2020), including both “new” submissions and “cross-lists” that appear on this page.
- Counting unit: Each unique arXiv identifier (e.g., 2001.xxxxx). Count each article once.

2) Time Window and Listing Source
- Time window: January 1–31, 2020.
- Source of record: arXiv monthly listing page for hep-lat, January 2020: https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2001. This page defines the complete set of “articles listed in January 2020.”

3) Definition of “PS Version Available”
An article is counted as having a PostScript (PS) version available if any of the following conditions are met on arXiv:
- The article’s “Other formats” page (https://arxiv.org/formats/{identifier}) lists a working link labeled “PostScript” or “PS”, typically pointing to:
  • https://arxiv.org/ps/{identifier}
  • https://arxiv.org/ps/{identifier}vN (versioned)
- The “Other formats” page lists a compressed PostScript link (gzipped), e.g. “PostScript (gzipped)” with URL patterns like:
  • https://arxiv.org/ps/{identifier}.gz
  • https://arxiv.org/ps/{identifier}vN.gz
- Occasionally, a direct PostScript link may appear on the abstract page; presence of such a link also qualifies.

Notes:
- Presence of the PostScript link on the formats page is sufficient to count as “available.” We will not require downloading to verify integrity unless a link clearly returns an error.
- Links to other formats (PDF, DVI, source) do not count toward PS availability unless a specific PostScript link is present.

4) Inclusions and Exclusions
- Include: All records shown on https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2001 (both primary hep-lat and cross-lists that appear on this page).
- Exclude: Items not appearing on that page; duplicate counting of the same arXiv identifier; withdrawn entries are included only if they appear on the January 2020 listing and have PS links available at the time of checking.

5) Handling Edge Cases
- Multiple versions: Count based on any version offering a PS link (if v1 lacks PS but v2 has PS, count as available; we are evaluating current availability when checked).
- Broken links: If a PS link is present but returns a clear error on attempt, document the case; default rule is to count presence unless broken links are systematically observed.
- Redirects: If PostScript links redirect to an equivalent resource (e.g., via versioned canonicalization), still count as PS available.

6) Operational Procedure (for subsequent steps)
- Step 1: Extract all arXiv IDs from https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2001.
- Step 2: For each ID, open https://arxiv.org/formats/{identifier} and check for presence of “PostScript” or “PostScript (gzipped)” links.
- Step 3: Aggregate binary availability per ID and compute total count; perform spot checks for link functionality as needed.
- Output: Provide final count and a brief methodology summary.

qa_pairs.question: Which specific turn was the primary cause for the 'FileNotFoundError' in Turn 8 by saving the HTML file to the root workspace instead of the expected 'raw' subdirectory?

qa_pairs.question: Before executing the code in Turn 9, what logic did the agent have to add to its path handling to ensure the script didn't fail a second time?

qa_pairs.question: According to the tool call in Turn 0, what are the allowed values for the 'mode' parameter in the file_saver function, and which one was used?

qa_pairs.question: Based on the methodology defined in 'criteria_hep-lat_ps_availability_step0.md', what specific label must a link have on the 'Other formats' page to be counted as a PostScript version?
qa_pairs.answer: The link must be labeled 'PostScript', 'PS', or 'PostScript (gzipped)'.

qa_pairs.question: What was the last step index that the agent successfully marked as 'completed' prior to Turn 14?

What was the state of the 'website_url' argument in the fetch_website_content call at Turn 3?
What was the primary technical failure that prevented the agent from extracting all 97 article IDs during its first successful script execution in Turn 9?
qa_pairs.answer: The agent saved a truncated version of the HTML content to its workspace. This manual truncation cut off the majority of the article list, leaving only 10 IDs available for the regex to find.

qa_pairs.question: Explain the observation at Turn 3 regarding why the agent fetched nearly 800 entries despite only needing articles from January 2020.
qa_pairs.answer: The agent confused Arxiv's URL naming convention. It treated the '2001' suffix as the year 2001, resulting in a fetch of 794 entries, rather than recognizing that '2001' in a monthly context represents the year 2020, month 01.
